seoTitle: How to Run a General Ledger Report in QuickBooks
metaDescription: Generate a full General Ledger report in QuickBooks Online, filter by date and account, and save your custom view for future audits.
intro: The General Ledger report gives you a chronological record of every transaction in your books, which is essential for tax prep and audits. This guide shows you how to run the report, adjust its date range and columns, and save your setup for next time.

steps.heading: Open the Reports section
steps.body: From the left-hand sidebar, click Reports to access the full reporting library.

steps.heading: Search for General Ledger
steps.body: Type 'General Ledger' into the 'Find report by name' search bar and select it from the dropdown.

steps.heading: Set your date range and accounting method
steps.body: Use the customization toolbar to define your financial period, then toggle between Cash and Accrual as needed.

steps.heading: Run the report
steps.body: Click Run Report to generate a complete multi-column ledger of your transactions.

steps.heading: Customize columns and filters
steps.body: Click Customize to open advanced filters and check or uncheck fields like Memo or Transaction Type to control what's displayed.

steps.heading: Save your custom view
steps.body: Click Save Customization to create a shortcut for this report configuration so you can access it quickly during future audits.

faq.question: What does a General Ledger report show?
faq.answer: It shows a complete chronological list of all your business transactions, including opening balances, net changes, and ending balances.

faq.question: Can I filter the report by a specific account or vendor?
faq.answer: Yes, the Customize panel lets you apply deep filters to isolate individual accounts, vendors, or transaction types.

faq.question: How do I keep this report handy for future use?
faq.answer: After customizing it, click Save Customization to create a dashboard shortcut you can access again without rebuilding the filters.

faq.question: Should I use cash or accrual for this report?
faq.answer: It depends on your reporting guidelines or what your accountant needs, and QuickBooks lets you toggle between both before running the report.
